Wed Oct  8 09:15:49 2025

walsh_transform_test():
  python version: 3.10.12
  numpy version:  1.26.4
  Test walsh_transform().

ffwt_test():
  ffwt() computes a fast Walsh transform.

     I        X(I)   Y=FFWT(X)/N  Z=FFWT(Y)/N

     0    0.476297    0.476021    0.476297

fwt_test():
  fwt() computes a fast Walsh transform.

     I        X(I)    Y=FWT(X)/N   Z=FWT(Y)/N

haar_test()
  haar() computes a Haar transform.
  hnorm() normalizes the transformed data.
  haar_inverse() computes an inverse Haar transform.

     I        X(I)    Y=HAAR(X)  Z=HNORM(Y)  W=haar_inverse(Z)

     0    0.479499    8.623143    0.538946    0.479499
     1    0.591866   -0.369464   -0.023091    0.591866
     2    0.306440    0.233841    0.029230    0.306440
     3    0.802536   -0.168375   -0.021047    0.802536
     4    0.676469   -0.037610   -0.009402    0.676469
     5    0.301162    0.008761    0.002190    0.301162
     6    0.590851    0.191799    0.047950    0.590851
     7    0.378018    0.060083    0.015021    0.378018
     8    0.953583   -0.112367   -0.056183    0.953583
     9    0.224298   -0.496096   -0.248048    0.224298
    10    0.744184    0.375307    0.187654    0.744184
    11    0.241899    0.212834    0.106417    0.241899
    12    0.373271    0.729285    0.364643    0.373271
    13    0.822940    0.502286    0.251143    0.822940
    14    0.760510   -0.449670   -0.224835    0.760510
    15    0.375618    0.384892    0.192446    0.375618

     I        X(I)    Y=HAAR(X)  Z=HNORM(Y)  W=haar_inverse(Z)

walsh_test():
  walsh() computes a fast Walsh transform.

     I        X(I)    Y=FWT(X)/N   Z=FWT(Y)/N

     0    0.113672    0.560859    0.113672
